description: Set the value of mpoint attributes. Individual attributes can be listed with the mpoint list command and all attributes can be visualized.

keywords.name: density
keywords.description: Mpoint density, where f > 0. By default, mpoint density is set to zero upon creation of an mpoint; a non-zero value is required for MPoint to properly compute mass properties and integrate the equation of motion on the background grid. An exception is thrown if an mpoint with zero density exists in the model when cycling starts. Density can also be set with the mpoint property command.

keywords.name: displacement
keywords.description: Accumulated mpoint displacement vector as a result of cycling.

keywords.name: displacement-x
keywords.description: The \(x\) -component of the accumulated mpoint displacement as a result of cycling.

keywords.name: displacement-y
keywords.description: The \(y\) -component of the accumulated mpoint displacement as a result of cycling.

keywords.name: displacement-z
keywords.description: The \(z\) -component of the accumulated mpoint displacement as a result of cycling.

keywords.name: pore-pressure
keywords.description: Pore pressure of mpoint. The mpoint stress state remains unchanged.

keywords.name: position
keywords.description: Location of mpoint.

keywords.name: position-x
keywords.description: The \(x\) -component of the location of mpoint.

keywords.name: position-y
keywords.description: The \(y\) -component of the location of mpoint.

keywords.name: position-z
keywords.description: The \(z\) -component of the location of mpoint.

keywords.name: strain-xx
keywords.description: The \(x\) -component of the mpoint strain.

keywords.name: strain-xy
keywords.description: The \(xy\) -component of the mpoint strain.

keywords.name: strain-xz
keywords.description: The \(xz\) -component of the mpoint strain.

keywords.name: strain-yy
keywords.description: The \(yy\) -component of the mpoint strain.

keywords.name: strain-yz
keywords.description: The \(yz\) -component of the mpoint strain.

keywords.name: strain-zz
keywords.description: The \(zz\) -component of the mpoint strain.

keywords.name: stress-xx
keywords.description: The \(x\) -component of the mpoint total stress.

keywords.name: stress-xy
keywords.description: The \(xy\) -component of the mpoint total stress.

keywords.name: stress-xz
keywords.description: The \(xz\) -component of the mpoint total stress.

keywords.name: stress-yy
keywords.description: The \(yy\) -component of the mpoint total stress.

keywords.name: stress-yz
keywords.description: The \(yz\) -component of the mpoint total stress.

keywords.name: stress-zz
keywords.description: The \(zz\) -component of the mpoint total stress.

keywords.name: velocity
keywords.description: Mpoint velocity vector.

keywords.name: velocity-x
keywords.description: The \(x\) -component of the mpoint velocity.

keywords.name: velocity-y
keywords.description: The \(y\) -component of the mpoint velocity.

keywords.name: velocity-z
keywords.description: The \(z\) -component of the mpoint velocity.

keywords.name: kwd:gradient
keywords.description: Apply a gradient to the scalar-value provided. The value specified for the main command is assumed to be applied at 0,0 (2D) or 0,0,0 (3D). For scalars quantities, a gradient vector is given that changes the value by the specified amount per unit length in x,y (and z and 3D) directions. For vector quantities, separate gradients must be given for each direction (e.g. gradient-x ) if needed. Examples are shown below.
